subject: Re: crash during refinement - IndexOutOfRangeException
body:
I suspect the CTF estimation failed for this tilt series for some reason, 
and we don't check for that or the item's deselection status when we bake 
the tilt series list into a data source. Will add more checks in the future.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, July 1, 2025 at 2:33:43 PM UTC-7 Mart Last wrote:

> Hi Dimitry,
>
> When I run the refinement with 1 GPU and --perdevice_refine 1, it does 
> consistently fail on 81. I'm not entirely sure which tilt series that is, 
> but among the refinement logs that were written within the minute prior to 
> the crash there is only one that shows an error:
>
> 2025-07-01 20:53:26.944 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
> #0, 23211 MB free:
> 2025-07-01 20:53:26.947 Loading tilt series and masks...
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.591 22891 MB after loading raw data of 
> 250531_l7p2_5.tomostar
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.644 Calculating spectral weights...
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.841 22887 MB after spectra estimation of 
> 250531_l7p2_5.tomostar
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.957 System.IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside 
> the bounds of the array.
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.957    at 
> Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass149_0.<PerformMultiParticleRefinement>b__19(Int32 
> t) in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.MPARefinement.cs:line 
> 1249
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.957    at Warp.Tools.Helper.ArrayOfFunction[T](Func`2 
> generator, Int32 n) in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/Tools/Helper.cs:line 
> 871
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.957    at 
> Warp.TiltSeries.PerformMultiParticleRefinement(String workingDirectory, 
> ProcessingOptionsMPARefine optionsMPA, Species[] allSpecies, DataSource 
> dataSource, Image gainRef, DefectModel defectMap, Action`1 
> progressCallback) in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.MPARefinement.cs:line 
> 1249
> 2025-07-01 20:53:27.957    at 
> WarpWorker.WarpWorker.EvaluateCommand(NamedSerializableObject Command) in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpWorker/WarpWorker.cs:line 
> 178
> 2025-07-01 20:53:38.064 10 seconds without heartbeat, exiting
>
> The warp_tiltseries/*.xml file for that one does indeed look bad, in the 
> way you predicted,  in comparison to the .xml files for tiltseries that 
> were included in previous refinements without issues. I am attaching the 
> bad and a normal .xml's for reference (the bad one is the 7 kb 
> 250531_l7p2_5.xml, l2p2_2.xml is good). 
>
> Just based on the file sizes all other .xml's seemed fine (>200 kB), so I 
> deleted the faulty one and replaced it with a different but correct version 
> of it that I found somewhere in warp_tiltseries/versions. Now the crash 
> doesn't occur :) 
> Would be interesting to know why exactly this bugged version of the .xml 
> gets written. I think it got generated when I created the population and it 
> is not a one off thing; I re-created this same population and species three 
> times under different names, and the same issue occurred each time. But the 
> required CTF grids do exist somewhere, because I do have these earlier 
> versions of 250531_l7p2_5.xml with the right CTF info...
>
> Best,
> Mart
> Op zondag 29 juni 2025 om 03:59:15 UTC+1 schreef teg...@gmail.com:
>
>> It's really odd this would fail at item #81 and not the first one. You've 
>> run CTF estimation on all of them, right? That's the only scenario I can 
>> think of where the CTF-related grids wouldn't be initialized correctly. If 
>> you go into the population folder's refinement_temp/logs, can you figure 
>> out which tilt series cause the crash? Does that tilt series' XML file 
>> contain GridCTF, GridCTFDefocusDelta, GridCTFDefocusAngle, GridCTFPhase 
>> nodes, and are their dimensions initialized to match the number of tilts in 
>> Z?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Friday, June 27, 2025 at 1:09:40 PM UTC-7 Mart Last wrote:
>>
>>> Hi,
>>>
>>> I am getting a persistent error during refinement of a new species. 
>>> Using MCore 2.0.0+87ad7adff287409f7391b986b71b8ac86910f5c9. Although the 
>>> initial check with MCore --population m/v.population --iter 0 works, when I 
>>> try to refine things go wrong. Am using a node with 4 RTX4090's.
>>>
>>> *MCore --population m/v.population --refine_particles*
>>>
>>> Loading population... Done
>>> Creating directories... Done
>>> Spawning workers... Done
>>> Preparing for refinement – this will take a few minutes per species
>>> Preparing refinement requisites...
>>> 1/1
>>> Performing refinement
>>> Preparing population for data source v...Done
>>> Loading gain reference for v... Done
>>> Refining all series in data source...
>>> 80/612Unhandled exception. System.Exception: BadRequest: 
>>> System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionResponseContent
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
>>> 232
>>>    at MCore.MCore.<>c__DisplayClass16_4.<DoProcessing>b__4(Int32 ifile, 
>>> Int32 threadID) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/MCore/MCore.cs:line 
>>> 474
>>>    at Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass51_0.<ForCPU>b__0(Object id) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/Tools/Helper.cs:line 
>>> 719
>>> Aborted (core dumped)
>>>
>>>
>>> *And the corresponding worker crash log:*
>>>
>>> System.IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside the bounds of the 
>>> array.
>>>    at 
>>> Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass149_0.<PerformMultiParticleRefinement>b__19(Int32 
>>> t) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.MPARefinement.cs:line 
>>> 1249
>>>    at Warp.Tools.Helper.ArrayOfFunction[T](Func`2 generator, Int32 n) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/Tools/Helper.cs:line 
>>> 871
>>>    at Warp.TiltSeries.PerformMultiParticleRefinement(String 
>>> workingDirectory, ProcessingOptionsMPARefine optionsMPA, Species[] 
>>> allSpecies, DataSource dataSource, Image gainRef, DefectModel defectMap, 
>>> Action`1 progressCallback) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.MPARefinement.cs:line 
>>> 1249
>>>    at WarpWorker.WarpWorker.EvaluateCommand(NamedSerializableObject 
>>> Command) in 
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpWorker/WarpWorker.cs:line 
>>> 843
>>>
>>> I found one earlier post that may be related (
>>> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/9IQFs1xZn3Y/m/ivPvKnBCBwAJ) but 
>>> that was with an older version. 
>>>
>>>
>>> It may be I'm doing something wrong but I am not sure what that might 
>>> be. Any ideas? Thanks for the help!
>>>
>>>
